data_found_other_installation_message.message:
Your profiles from another Delta Chat installation are not accessible - different installations store their data in separate locations.

To keep existing profiles:
1. Quit now
2. Open the other Delta Chat - if needed, reinstall it from %1$s
3. Back up each profile at "Settings/Chats"
4. Relaunch this version
5. Restore the backups using "I already have a profile"

Continue without your previous profiles?

key_management_desc.message: View and manage encryption keys for your account and contacts

key_management_pin_desc.message: Force use of this specific key for encryption (not yet supported by core)

key_management_unpin_desc.message: Remove manual key override and restore automatic key selection (not yet supported by core)

key_management_import_desc.message: Replace the current account key with an imported PGP private key (experimental)

key_management_import_warning_body.message:
Importing a private key is destructive and experimental. Please read carefully:

• Your current account key will be REPLACED.
• Verified (green checkmark) groups will break and show 'non-verified encryption' warnings.
• Only ASCII-armored PGP keys (.asc) without a passphrase are supported.
• Only RSA or Ed25519 keys are guaranteed to work.
• Future Delta Chat updates may not migrate imported keys correctly.
• This feature was removed from the official client for these reasons.

Only proceed if you understand the consequences.

key_management_import_confirm_body.message:
Import key from:
%1$s

This will REPLACE your current account key. This action cannot be undone.

key_management_cannot_replace_body.message:
This account already has an encryption key, and Delta Chat Core does not allow replacing it.

Reason: changing the key would break verified groups and encrypted conversations you are already part of. The underlying protocols (Autocrypt / SecureJoin) have no way to tell peers that your key changed.

To use a custom PGP key:

1. Create a new profile (below)
2. Import the key BEFORE logging in
3. Then configure the email transport

Make sure the key's email matches the address you will log in with.

key_management_cannot_replace_current.message: Current key fingerprint on this account:

key_management_export_desc.message: Export the account's private and public keys as PGP .asc files (experimental)

key_management_export_warning_body.message:
Exporting your private key has serious risks. Please read carefully:

• The exported .asc files are NOT encrypted — anyone with access to them can decrypt your messages (past and future).
• There is no revocation — once a copy leaves your device you cannot take it back.
• File permissions are not enforced; store on an encrypted disk or password manager.
• In browser mode the files are written to a server-side directory and kept briefly after download; do not leave them there.

Only continue if you understand the consequences.

key_management_export_confirm_body.message:
Export keys to:
%1$s

Multiple .asc files will be written. Files are UNENCRYPTED.

key_management_export_success_browser_fi.message:
A key file is ready to download:
%1$s

key_management_export_partial_browser_fi.message:
The export failed, but a key file was already written to the server:
%1$s

Download it now? (Clicking download will also trigger server-side cleanup 10s after download completes.)

key_management_export_success_browser_em.message: Export finished, but no files were reported. Check logs.
